Title: Direct and indirect responses of the Arabidopsis transcriptome to an induced increase in trehalose 6-phosphate

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sucrose-status signal linking metabolism, growth, and development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in companion cells, phloem parenchyma, and guard cells in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous TPS1 transcript is repressed when Tre6P is elevated, indicating rewiring of Tre6P metabolism rather than being the direct driver of the induced phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS1 encodes the main endogenous enzyme responsible for Tre6P synthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. TPS1 transcript decreased by >2-fold after induced bacterial TPS expression. 3. Its response was assigned to CRF G2, indicating the repression is interpreted as indirect, likely related to sucrose decline rather than direct Tre6P signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inducible overexpression system; RNA-seq transcript profiling; CRF-based response classification; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 22-d plants in long-day conditions; 2% ethanol spray at ZT0.5; rosettes harvested 4–6 h later in light
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to Tre6P biosynthesis, TPS1 was not the transgene used to elevate Tre6P and is not the primary manipulated gene in Results.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for understanding endogenous Tre6P homeostasis; manipulation should consider feedback repression and sucrose-Tre6P coupling

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: iTPS lines 29.2 and 31.3 in Columbia-0 background
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pAlcA ethanol-inducible promoter controlled by p35S:alcR
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to glucose-6-phosphate to form trehalose-6-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal metabolite that reports sucrose status and coordinates metabolism, growth, and development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein detectable by 2 h after ethanol induction and increased further at 4 and 6 h
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced otsA expression raised Tre6P about 4-fold, decreased sucrose, and triggered widespread transcriptome remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ethanol-inducible expression of bacterial otsA in Arabidopsis increased Tre6P significantly by 4 h and to ~4-fold by 6 h. 2. Tre6P elevation was accompanied by reduced sucrose and broad metabolic shifts. 3. Over 13,000 transcripts changed significantly and >5,000 showed ≥2-fold changes, enabling dissection of direct Tre6P versus indirect sugar-related responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inducible transgenic expression; protein detection; Tre6P metabolite quantification by LC-MS/MS; sucrose assay; RNA-seq; comparison with carbon response factor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for experimental elevation of Tre6P; not established as endogenous rate-limiting step in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; 2% ethanol spray at beginning of day; harvest at 2, 4, 6 h; rosette tissue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Concomitant sucrose decrease and broader metabolic perturbation create indirect transcriptomic effects; constitutive overexpression previously caused strong growth/development phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the main experimentally manipulated core gene in the paper. Source species and applied species are different and should not be merged.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful tool to modulate Tre6P signaling, but engineering must manage indirect effects on sucrose, central metabolism, and growth

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SNF1-related protein kinase catalytic alpha subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Kinase-mediated transcriptional and metabolic starvation signaling; function inferred to be inhibited by elevated Tre6P
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Starvation-induced gene set including DIN genes, bZIP11/bZIP63-associated outputs, ribosome biogenesis and biosynthesis/growth genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many starvation-related genes and represses many biosynthesis/growth-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon starvation signaling; repression/activation of biosynthesis, growth, and nutrient-responsive transcriptional programs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sucrose-status signal that inhibits starvation signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated Tre6P produced transcript changes largely reciprocal to transient SnRK1α1 overexpression, supporting in vivo inhibition of SnRK1 signaling by Tre6P
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. About 500 SnRK1-induced starvation genes were largely repressed by elevated Tre6P. 2. For transcripts assigned to direct Tre6P response group G1, iTPS and transient SnRK1α1 overexpression showed strong negative correlation. 3. Tre6P therefore acts as a major inhibitor of SnRK1-dependent starvation outputs, especially for induced targets.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics versus published transient SnRK1α1 overexpression dataset; CRF deconvolution; marker-gene comparisons
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key mediator through which Tre6P exerts major transcriptional effects on carbon-starvation and growth programs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Tre6P-SnRK1 network includes feedback/intermeshed regulation; increased SnRK1 expression reportedly dampens Tre6P response to sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Catalytic subunit of heterotrimeric SnRK1 complex with β and βγ regulatory subunits
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SnRK1β1; SnRK1β2; SnRK1β3; SnRK1βγ; TPS class II proteins; FLZ proteins
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tre6P elevation by inducible bacterial TPS in light period
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth/starvation balance may be altered; biosynthesis/growth outputs are more complex than starvation outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SnRK1α1 itself was mainly used through comparison to prior overexpression data rather than direct manipulation in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strategic node for tuning carbon allocation and growth-defense balance, but manipulation may cause broad pleiotropy

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional modulation of SnRK1 complex composition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly alters SnRK1 outputs by changing complex composition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript decreased >2-fold by 6 h in response to elevated Tre6P, indicating Tre6P modifies SnRK1 subunit composition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK1β1 was assigned to G1. 2. Its transcript decreased strongly after Tre6P induction. 3. This suggests Tre6P reshapes SnRK1 complex composition in addition to inhibiting SnRK1 activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; CRF assignment; comparison with SnRK1α1-response datasets
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Represents a direct route by which Tre6P may remodel SnRK1 signaling specificity
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of intermeshed Tre6P-SnRK1 regulatory loop
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Regulatory subunit of SnRK1 heterotrimeric complex
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethanol-induced Tre6P increase in rosettes under light
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing SnRK1 complex composition may broadly alter stress and growth responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer that in wild type, induction under carbon starvation may reflect declining Tre6P and activated SnRK1.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to fine-tune SnRK1 outputs, but likely highly pleiotropic

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional modulation of SnRK1 complex composition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect via altered SnRK1 complex assembly/specification
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal metabolite coordinating carbon availability with growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Showed weak induction and G1 assignment after Tre6P elevation, suggesting a Tre6P-dependent shift toward β2-containing SnRK1 complexes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK1β2 transcript increased weakly in response to elevated Tre6P. 2. It was assigned to G1, consistent with a direct Tre6P-linked response. 3. Together with β1 repression, this implies changed SnRK1 subunit balance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to Tre6P-dependent rewiring of SnRK1 signaling rather than direct metabolite synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad signaling effects expected if manipulated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Response magnitude was weaker than β1 repression.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for redesigning carbon-signaling output, likely best used in combination approaches

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional modulation of SnRK1 complex composition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Weakly induced and assigned to G1 after Tre6P elevation, consistent with Tre6P modifying SnRK1 regulatory composition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK1βγ transcript showed a weak increase after Tre6P induction. 2. It was among regulatory subunits assigned to G1. 3. Findings support a role for Tre6P in altering SnRK1 complex composition.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports Tre6P-dependent reprogramming of SnRK1 activity/output
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to broad kinase-network effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SnRK1β3 response was inconsistent; βγ response was highlighted as weak increase.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier of SnRK1 function, but not yet a stand-alone engineering target

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Expression repression by elevated Tre6P; proposed modulation of SnRK1 function
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Elevated Tre6P; likely via SnRK1 inhibition; carbon status
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect; class II TPS proteins are proposed to interact with and inhibit SnRK1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal metabolite integrating sucrose status with growth and metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TPS8-11 transcripts were repressed >2-fold and assigned to G1, indicating direct Tre6P-linked rewiring of the Tre6P/SnRK1 signaling network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Class II TPS genes TPS8-11 were strongly repressed by elevated Tre6P. 2. They were assigned to G1, unlike TPS1. 3. Because class II TPS proteins are thought to modulate SnRK1, their repression may provide feedforward amplification within the Tre6P-SnRK1 network.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; CRF grouping; integration with published protein-interaction evidence discussed by authors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important secondary regulators that may amplify or tune Tre6P inhibition of SnRK1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed feedforward amplification within Tre6P-SnRK1 network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Reported/argued to physically interact with SnRK1 in published evidence cited by authors
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating these genes may alter starvation responses and growth under changing carbon supply
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors distinguish these class II TPS genes from enzymatic TPS1; they are considered regulatory rather than catalytic here.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising modulators for fine-tuning Tre6P/SnRK1 signaling without directly forcing Tre6P synthesis

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction/repression in response to carbon status and Tre6P; negative regulation of SnRK1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Carbon status; elevated Tre6P; likely SnRK1 status
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect through modulation of SnRK1 and its interaction with TORC
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon signaling, growth-related signaling, SnRK1-TORC cross-talk
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated Tre6P altered expression of FLZ genes, supporting Tre6P-dependent remodeling of SnRK1 regulatory circuitry
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLZ proteins are negative regulators of SnRK1 and are implicated in SnRK1-TORC interaction. 2. Their expression is regulated by carbon status and a subset is consistent with regulation by Tre6P inhibition of SnRK1. 3. Tre6P therefore likely affects SnRK1 outputs partly through FLZ-family regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome comparison; CRF analysis; integration with published functional evidence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as part of the regulatory layer through which Tre6P shapes broader carbon-signaling outputs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely embedded in network feedback between Tre6P, SnRK1, and TORC
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Implicated in SnRK1 regulatory interactions and SnRK1-TORC crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad signaling perturbation likely if manipulated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific FLZ member names were not provided in the main text excerpt; extraction kept at family/subset level.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; ATH1 microarray; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tools for indirect tuning of carbon signaling once family-member specificity is resolved

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: S1-type bZIP transcription factor with sucrose-regulated uORFs
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Translational control by sucrose; transcriptional low-energy response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low sucrose/energy status; uORF-mediated sucrose control; interaction with C-type bZIPs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates starvation responses and inhibits growth when translated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Many bZIP11-responsive genes overlapped with iTPS response; for many shared targets, elevated Tre6P acted oppositely to bZIP11 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The iTPS response overlaps substantially with published bZIP11 overexpression responses. 2. Many shared transcripts assigned to G1 responded oppositely to elevated Tre6P and bZIP11 overexpression. 3. This supports parallel/antagonistic control of low-carbon transcriptional programs by Tre6P and bZIP11-related signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics with published bZIP11 overexpression dataset
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important comparator/regulatory node linking sucrose deprivation to starvation transcriptional responses antagonized by Tre6P
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose status and induced Tre6P elevation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation can inhibit growth and promote starvation programs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a regulatory comparator rather than primary manipulated gene in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; comparative use of published overexpression transcriptomics; transcriptome accession E-MTAB-12783
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May help rebalance growth versus starvation/stress signaling, but direct use likely pleiotropic
